author: felicepantaleo
body: The per-axis radius estimate is a variance over the cluster cells, computed in float against the stored log-weighted cluster position. For clusters whose cells all share one coordinate (e.g. a vertical column of hexagonal silicon cells, about 2500 per TTbar PU200 event) the true variance along that axis is zero and the one-pass formula subtracts two equal rounding residues, landing below zero about half the time; the sqrt then yields a NaN radius whose comparisons are silently false in the seed and density decisions.
title: Clamp the CLUE3D cluster radius variance at zero
